name: host-context-observed-persists
description: The iframe-runtime captures `McpUiHostContext` from the `ui/initialize` response, trims it IFRAME-SIDE to `HostContextProjection` (`projectHostContext` in `@ggui-ai/protocol` — `theme`/`styles` are deliberately not projection fields; they flow through ggui's theming pipeline), and echoes the projection via the `host_context_observed` Client→Server message. The server's obligation is purely stateful: persist `payload.hostContext` onto `GguiSession.hostContext` VERBATIM — the first-party handler patches the received projection as-is, with no server-side re-projection — so `ggui_handshake` / `ggui_consume` surface it to the agent on later turns. That is why this fixture's `expected` is exactly the projection it authors. There is no synchronous response frame, so a `no-op` (wire-silence) assertion would wrongly certify a server that drops the message; the kit grades the obligation by reading `hostContext` back through `ConformanceHost.readSessionField` after the observation window and deep-equalling it against `expected`. Hosts without that introspection seam SKIP — never a weakened pass.
